notes: Issue #3538 is an enhancement request to add WheelEvent handling to the Tizen SKTouchHandler. Code inspection confirms the Tizen handler (SKTouchHandler.cs) only subscribes to view.TouchEvent with no WheelEvent support, while the Windows handler already implements PointerWheelChanged with SKTouchAction.WheelChanged and WheelDelta. The shared API surface (SKTouchAction.WheelChanged, SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta) already exists. Cannot verify on Tizen — no Tizen workload or device/emulator available in this environment.

reproductionSteps.description: Check dotnet environment for Tizen workload availability
reproductionSteps.output: Installed workloads: android, ios, maccatalyst, macos, maui, tvos, wasm-tools. No tizen workload found.

reproductionSteps.description: Inspect Tizen SKTouchHandler source for wheel event support
reproductionSteps.output: SKTouchHandler only subscribes to view.TouchEvent (line 29). No WheelEvent subscription or handler exists. Only handles PointStateType: Down, Up, Motion, Leave, Interrupted.

reproductionSteps.description: Verify Windows SKTouchHandler has wheel support as reference
Verify shared SKTouchAction.WheelChanged and WheelDelta API exists
Attempt to create Tizen MAUI project — blocked by missing workload
